mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in future rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving equity valuations higher through improved corporate earnings prospects over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost corporate margins and drive SPX returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger equity earnings growth and positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when INDPRO shows positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% quarterly decline in real sales (RSAFS) signals weak consumer demand, leading to SPX underperformance as corporate revenue expectations decline over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 5% in 40%+ of instances where RSAFS declines by >10%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust employment, increasing consumer spending and driving SPX returns positively over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate tightening monetary policy and inflation, leading to SPX underperformance as valuation multiples contract over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise by >0.25%.
